mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate over a 20-day window signals tightening financial conditions and a higher discount rate for equities, which should reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive more often than negative after FEDFUNDS rises over rolling 20-day windows, or if the sign of the effect is positive in most rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger nominal household spending, supporting revenue expectations and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS accelerations fail to coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered periods produce negative median forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor-market deterioration, lower earnings durability, and weaker S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are not materially worse after UNRATE threshold crossings, or if the hit rate turns positive in more than 40% of threshold events.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while HOUST is falling, industrial supply remains firm but construction demand weakens, a combination that signals margin pressure and softer S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up/HOUST-down condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses explanatory power in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST below its recent monthly trend indicates a cooling housing demand impulse that reduces cyclical growth expectations and lowers S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by neutral or positive median 60-day SPX returns, or if the directional hit rate stays below 45% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with elevated policy rates implies the Federal Reserve is likely to stay restrictive, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE/high-FEDFUNDS regime does not show weaker 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if returns are positive in most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-hike environment, an increase in PAYEMS signals resilient payroll growth and sustained income support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as recession fears fade.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged or lower over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in the low-rate-stability regime, or if the sign reverses during repeated non-recessionary months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy rates fall and equity discount rates stay too loose, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up/policy-lag episodes do not outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS catches up to inflation.
